title: Conversation Practice — Basics of English
subtitle: 25 real-life conversation scenarios with prompts and model answers
description: Har scenario mein Hindi cue hai aur model answers hain. Practice karo: pehle khud try karo, phir model answer dekho. Isse speaking confidence badhegi!
howToUse: Hindi cue padho aur samjho ki kya bolna hai
Pehle apne aap se English mein bolne ki koshish karo
Phir model answers dekho aur compare karo
Partner ke saath role-play karo ya khud record karo
tipOfTheDay: Roz ek scenario practice karo — 25 din mein conversation confident ho jayega!

scenarios.cue: Aap ek party mein hain. Koi naya aadmi aaya hai. Apna parichay do.
prompts: Tell your name and where you are from
modelAnswers: Hello! My name is Rahul. I am from Lucknow.
I work as a software engineer at TCS in Noida.
I love playing cricket and reading books.
scenarios.grammarFocus: Simple Present Tense + I am / I work / I study

scenarios.cue: Aap ek coffee shop mein hain. Waiter se coffee order karna hai.
modelAnswers: Can I have the menu, please?
I would like a cappuccino, please. Medium size.
How much does it cost?
scenarios.grammarFocus: Do/Does questions + Would like (polite request)

scenarios.cue: Ek doosrhe se apne parivaar ke baare mein batao.
prompts: How many people are in your family?
modelAnswers: There are four people in my family.
My father is a teacher and my mother is a homemaker.
I have one brother. He studies in Class 10.
scenarios.grammarFocus: He/She + verb+s + My/His/Her possessives

scenarios.cue: Apne shahar ke baare mein batao — kya khaas hai.
prompts: Name your city and what it is famous for
Tell one thing you like about it
modelAnswers: I live in Jaipur. It is famous for its pink buildings and forts.
The weather is hot in summer and cool in winter.
I love the street food here. The dal baati churma is delicious!
scenarios.grammarFocus: Simple Present for facts + adjectives

scenarios.cue: Apni daily routine baatao — subah se raat tak.
modelAnswers: I wake up at 6 AM. I brush my teeth, take a shower, and eat breakfast.
I go to college from 9 to 4. I study English in the evening.
I eat dinner at 8 PM and sleep at 10:30 PM.
scenarios.grammarFocus: Simple Present for habits + Time expressions

scenarios.cue: Apne hobbies ke baare mein batao.
prompts: Describe how often you do it
modelAnswers: My main hobby is reading books. I love fiction and self-help books.
I read for about 30 minutes every night before sleeping.
I like reading because it improves my vocabulary and relaxes my mind.
scenarios.grammarFocus: Simple Present + Frequency adverbs + Because clauses

scenarios.cue: Apne kaam ya studies ke baare mein batao.
prompts: What do you like about your work/studies?
modelAnswers: I am a second-year BCA student.
I study at DU (Delhi University). I attend online and offline classes.
I like computer programming. I find it challenging and creative.
usefulPhrases: I study at / I work at...
scenarios.grammarFocus: I am + profession/student + I find + adjective

scenarios.cue: Tumhe kisi se madad maangni hai — politely.
prompts: Ask for help finding a place
modelAnswers: Excuse me, can you help me? I am looking for the nearest pharmacy.
Sorry, could you say that again, please? I don't understand.
Thank you so much! You are very helpful. I really appreciate it.
scenarios.grammarFocus: Can/Could for polite requests + I am looking for + I don't understand

scenarios.cue: Dost ke saath weekend ke plan banana.
prompts: Agree on a time and place
modelAnswers: Hey! Do you want to go to the movies this Saturday?
Which movie do you want to watch? I like action movies.
Let's meet at the mall at 4 PM. I'll book the tickets online.
scenarios.grammarFocus: Do you want to + V1 | Let's + V1 | Which + noun

scenarios.cue: Apni food preferences ke baare mein batao.
prompts: What do you like to eat?
modelAnswers: I love North Indian food — dal makhani, butter chicken, and naan.
I don't eat junk food much. I try to eat healthy.
My favorite restaurant is Bikanervala. It has delicious vegetarian food at good prices.
scenarios.grammarFocus: I love/like + noun | I don't eat + noun | It has + noun

scenarios.cue: Apne best friend ya family member ko describe karo.
modelAnswers: My best friend Aarav is tall and slim. He has short black hair and brown eyes.
He is very funny and kind. He always makes me laugh. He never gets angry.
He works as a graphic designer at a startup in Hyderabad.
scenarios.grammarFocus: He/She + is/has + adjective | He/She + verb+s | He always/never + verb+s

scenarios.cue: Apni health aur exercise habits ke baare mein batao.
prompts: How often do you do it?
What do you eat to stay healthy?
modelAnswers: I go for a run every morning. I also do yoga three times a week.
I run for about 30 minutes. After running, I do stretching exercises.
I eat healthy food — vegetables, fruits, and protein. I avoid oily and fried food.
usefulPhrases: I go for a run / walk
Three times a week / every day
scenarios.grammarFocus: Frequency expressions + I try to + V1 | Simple Present for habits

scenarios.cue: Clothes ki dukaan mein — koi shirt khareedni hai.
prompts: Ask if they have what you want
modelAnswers: Do you have formal shirts in white or blue?
I usually wear a medium size. How much does this one cost?
I like this one. It is good quality and the price is reasonable. I'll take it.
scenarios.grammarFocus: Do you have + noun? | How much + does + it/this + cost?

scenarios.cue: Doctor ke paas jaana hai — apni problem batao.
prompts: Tell how long you have had the problem
modelAnswers: Doctor, I have a fever, a sore throat, and a headache.
I have had this fever since yesterday morning. My throat hurts a lot.
What medicine do you recommend? Do I need to take antibiotics?
scenarios.grammarFocus: I have + a/an + symptom | Since/For + duration | Simple Present for facts

scenarios.cue: Apne hometown ke baare mein detail mein batao.
prompts: Where is it? What is it known for?
Compare it to where you live now
modelAnswers: My hometown is Varanasi. It is one of the oldest cities in the world. It is famous for the Ganga river, the ghats, and its temples.
The people are very traditional and religious. The culture is rich in music, dance, and festivals. The food is also amazing — especially the chaat and lassi.
I live in Delhi now. Delhi is bigger and more modern. But Varanasi has a unique spiritual atmosphere that Delhi doesn't have.
usefulPhrases: It is known for / famous for...
scenarios.grammarFocus: Simple Present for facts + Comparatives (bigger, more modern) + It has

scenarios.cue: Kisi problem ko explain karna — clearly aur politely.
modelAnswers: I have a problem with my internet connection. It disconnects every few minutes.
This is very frustrating because I work from home. I attend online meetings and need a stable connection.
Can you please check the connection? Is there a technical issue in my area?
scenarios.grammarFocus: I have + a problem | Because + reason | Can you + V1?

scenarios.cue: Apni favorite book ya movie ke baare mein batao.
prompts: Name the book/movie and the genre
modelAnswers: My favorite book is 'The Alchemist' by Paulo Coelho. It is a fiction novel about following your dreams.
It is about a young shepherd who travels across the world to find treasure. But in the end, he discovers the real treasure is within himself.
I recommend it because it is inspiring and teaches you to follow your dreams. The language is simple and beautiful.
scenarios.grammarFocus: Simple Present for plot summaries + Because + reason

scenarios.cue: Apne future plans ke baare mein batao (use Simple Present for near future plans).
prompts: What do you plan to do after college?
Where do you want to work?
modelAnswers: After college, I plan to work in a tech company as a software developer.
I want to work at Google or Microsoft. I know it is competitive, but I work hard every day to improve my skills.
In 5 years, I want to be a senior developer and lead a team. I also want to complete my MBA.
usefulPhrases: I work on... every day to...
scenarios.grammarFocus: I plan to/want to + V1 | Simple Present for ongoing habits that support future

scenarios.cue: Current events ke baare mein simple conversation.
prompts: Name a current event you know
modelAnswers: India wins the cricket World Cup! It is a great achievement for the entire country.
I think it is wonderful. The players work so hard. They deserve this success. Cricket brings India together.
What do you think? Do you follow cricket?
scenarios.grammarFocus: I think + sentence | Do you + V1? | Simple Present for opinions

scenarios.cue: Kisi ko directions batao — apne ghar se nearest market tak.
prompts: Tell them to go straight / turn
modelAnswers: Go straight on this road for about 200 meters.
When you see a big temple on your left, turn right. There is a McDonald's on the corner — you can't miss it.
The market is just 5 minutes walk from there. It is a blue building with a red sign.
scenarios.grammarFocus: Imperative sentences (Go, Turn, Walk) + Simple Present for landmarks

scenarios.cue: Kisi se galti ho gayi — apologize karna hai sincerely.
modelAnswers: I am so sorry. I am really sorry for missing our meeting yesterday.
I had an urgent issue at home. I try to always be on time, but yesterday it was impossible.
I understand if you are upset. Can we reschedule? I make sure it doesn't happen again.
scenarios.grammarFocus: Simple Present for commitments + I understand if + clause

scenarios.cue: Technology ke baare mein apni thoughts share karo.
prompts: What technology do you use daily?
modelAnswers: I use my smartphone every day for almost everything — communication, work, entertainment, and learning.
It helps me stay connected with family and friends. I also use apps to learn English and manage my time.
But one downside is that people spend too much time on social media. It distracts them from real work and relationships.
scenarios.grammarFocus: Simple Present for habits + It helps me + V1 | One downside is + that clause

scenarios.cue: Apna favorite Indian festival describe karo.
prompts: When does it happen and what is it about?
modelAnswers: My favorite festival is Diwali. It is the festival of lights.
It happens every year in October or November. It celebrates the return of Lord Rama to Ayodhya after 14 years of exile.
People clean their homes, buy new clothes, light diyas, burst crackers, and eat sweets. They also give gifts to family and friends.
scenarios.grammarFocus: Simple Present for facts + People + V1 (plural no 's') + It happens

scenarios.cue: Indian education system ke baare mein thoughts share karo.
prompts: What do you like about the Indian education system?
modelAnswers: India has very good engineering and medical colleges. IITs and AIIMS are world-class institutions. Many Indian students succeed globally.
But I think there is too much focus on marks and exams. Students memorize, but they don't always understand deeply. Creative thinking is sometimes ignored.
Schools need more practical learning. Teachers need to encourage questions and discussion. Students need to learn how to think, not just what to think.
scenarios.grammarFocus: I think + sentence | Too much + uncountable noun | Need to + V1

scenarios.cue: Office mein kisi colleague se kaam ke baare mein baat karo.
modelAnswers: Hey Priya! What project do you work on currently?
I see you look busy. Do you need any help? I complete my tasks early today.
When is your deadline? I think we can finish this together if we divide the work.
usefulPhrases: What project do you work on?
scenarios.grammarFocus: Do you + V1? | Simple Present for current status | I think + we can
